exemplar.title: Do the ever-improving tools of an area of knowledge always result in improved knowledge? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: Do the ever-improving tools of an area of knowledge always result in improved knowledge? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

strengths: Maintains a clear and sustained focus on the prescribed title throughout, consistently returning to the central knowledge question.
Employs a range of concrete examples (HPLC, Therac-25, Transkribus and Marxist labour history) that are relevant and generally well integrated into the argument.
Demonstrates an emerging awareness of alternative perspectives, for instance through invocation of Occamâs razor and historiansâ scepticism toward AI.
weaknesses: Analysis remains at times more descriptive than critical, with insufficient explicit linkage to TOK concepts such as reliability, validity and interpretation.
Depth of evaluation of counter-claims and the implications of arguments is limited and would benefit from more rigorous exploration.
Organization is at moments uneven, with abrupt transitions and incomplete paragraph development that slightly undermine coherence.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the essay exhibits a sustained and focused engagement with the prescribed titleâsupported by well-chosen examples and acknowledgment of alternative viewpointsâit falls just short of the very highest standard due to its largely descriptive analysis and occasional organizational lapses. These minor weaknesses prevent a perfect mark, but the studentâs clear command of the material and effective use of evidence merit a high level of achievement. Assigning 9 out of 10 aligns with the official examinerâs mark while reflecting the balance of strong focus and illustrative examples against the need for deeper critical analysis and tighter structure.

annotations.comment: Acknowledging Occamâs razor as a counterpoint shows emerging awareness of alternative perspectives and adds nuance to the discussion of tool complexity.
The critique of AI reliability is general. Provide concrete instances of mis-transcriptions or model errors to substantiate claims about potential distortions in historical knowledge.

The studentâs thesis is stated broadly (âNS and History differâ¦â). Refine the thesis to specify the exact claim structure, indicating how each area of knowledge will demonstrate when and why improved tools do or do not yield improved knowledge.

âImproved knowledgeâ is defined in general terms but not linked to TOK concepts like reliability, validity or interpretation. The student should anchor definitions in TOK terminology to sharpen the analysis.

The Therac-25 example is compelling but lacks explicit linkage to knowledge-production concepts. Analyze how design flaws undermined tool reliability and discuss the impact on acceptance of medical knowledge.

Listing auxiliary sciences (paleography, diplomatics) is valid, but the student should connect these methods to how historians validate authenticity and interpretation, linking to TOK themes of evidence and perspective.
The transition to the history section is abrupt. Add clear signposting to guide the reader and link back to the central knowledge question of the essay.
The Marxist lens example is strong but would benefit from acknowledging historiographical debate. Evaluate a non-Marxist critique to show deeper awareness of alternative frameworks.

The discussion of Transkribus highlights efficiency but needs precise connection to TOK concepts of representation and interpretation. Explain how automation alters the historianâs role in constructing knowledge.
The introduction of HPLC is abrupt and the quotation cuts off mid-sentence. Integrate the example more smoothly and complete the citation, ensuring the connection to your knowledge claim is explicit.

The student attributes enhanced âvalidityâ to HPLC but does not distinguish validity from reliability. Clarify these concepts and explain why HPLCâs increased sensitivity supports reliability rather than validity of results.

The invocation of Occamâs razor is interesting but the evaluation remains superficial. The student should explore the implications of selecting simpler explanations on scientific progress and whether simplicity always leads to better knowledge.
This paragraph fragment ends abruptly. Ensure each paragraph is complete and clearly develops one idea before moving to the next to maintain coherence.
The conclusion outlines differences but does not consider broader implications for the evolution of knowledge or how these insights might inform future tool design or usage.
The conclusion restates key points but never directly answers whether ever-improving tools always yield improved knowledge. Address this central question explicitly to close the argument.
Citation formatting is inconsistent (missing parenthetical details, incomplete quotes). Adopt a consistent referencing style throughout to strengthen credibility.
The studentâs introduction uses clear examples from both natural sciences and history, demonstrating breadth and an ability to select relevant cases for comparison.
The statistic âdetection percentagesâ¦ increased more than 25%â is stated without discussing its epistemic significance. Explain how this data point translates into improved knowledge.
Ethical considerations are omitted from the Therac-25 case. Discuss how ethical failures influence the credibility of scientific knowledge and public trust.
The use of G.D.H. Coleâs Marxist framework effectively illustrates how theoretical tools can guide historical investigations and shape knowledge claims.
Some informal phrasing and minor grammatical errors (e.g., âdo often have aâ) weaken the academic tone. Revise for precision and formality.
The conclusion effectively summarizes the main examples and reiterates the comparative focus between the natural sciences and history.
The section on AI concludes with a generic warning. Propose concrete steps for integrating AI tools responsibly alongside traditional methods to enhance historical inquiry.

The conclusion mentions varying alignments with methodological objectives but doesnât evaluate the validity of these objectives. Reflect on how different aims shape what counts as knowledge improvement.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content:
The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
